text:
A limit order lets you set the price you're willing to accept — it waits in the market until someone matches it at that price or better, or it expires. A market order executes immediately against available orders already in the market, at whatever price is currently offered.

Use a limit order when you're willing to wait for a specific price. Use a market order when you want to fill right now and price isn't the constraint.

On Novig's peer-to-peer exchange, both work the same way — you're matched against other users, not a house — but the timing and certainty trade off: limit orders are cheaper to place (no commission either way) but may not fill; market orders fill faster but at the market price at that moment.

Novig Support
